lines.line: # Introduction to the Interview

> The speaker introduces herself as Tiffany Sanford, a librarian at the North Hollywood regional branch of the Los Angeles Public Library, and sets the stage for the interview with Arthur Pond, who is present for the discussion.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Interview
metadata.synopsis: The speaker introduces herself as Tiffany Sanford, a librarian at the North Hollywood regional branch of the Los Angeles Public Library, and sets the stage for the interview with Arthur Pond, who is present for the discussion.

lines.line: # Early Career and Union Involvement

> Arthur Pond recounts the beginning of his career at LAPL as a young adult librarian and his subsequent transfers within the system. He also discusses his early involvement with the union, leading to his eventual role as union president.

metadata.title: Early Career and Union Involvement
metadata.synopsis: Arthur Pond recounts the beginning of his career at LAPL as a young adult librarian and his subsequent transfers within the system. He also discusses his early involvement with the union, leading to his eventual role as union president.

lines.line: # Senior Librarian Exam and Promotion

> The speaker describes taking the senior librarian's exam while ill, the rumors about his score, and the eventual revelation of his actual ranking. Despite the initial disappointment, he later receives a promotion to a senior librarian position.

metadata.title: Senior Librarian Exam and Promotion
metadata.synopsis: The speaker describes taking the senior librarian's exam while ill, the rumors about his score, and the eventual revelation of his actual ranking. Despite the initial disappointment, he later receives a promotion to a senior librarian position.

lines.line: # Hyde Park Branch and Union Presidency

> The speaker shares his experiences at the Hyde Park branch, including the impact of the LA riots and his tenure as union president. He reflects on the challenges and successes during this period of his career.

metadata.title: Hyde Park Branch and Union Presidency
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares his experiences at the Hyde Park branch, including the impact of the LA riots and his tenure as union president. He reflects on the challenges and successes during this period of his career.

lines.line: # The Great Migration and North Hollywood Retirement

> The speaker talks about the staff shuffling known as the Great Migration and his eventual retirement from the North Hollywood branch. He reflects on the changes in the library system and his own career trajectory.

metadata.title: The Great Migration and North Hollywood Retirement
metadata.synopsis: The speaker talks about the staff shuffling known as the Great Migration and his eventual retirement from the North Hollywood branch. He reflects on the changes in the library system and his own career trajectory.

lines.line: # Service to Shut-Ins and Hyde Park

> Arthur Pond talks about his role in the Municipal Reference Library and his transition to working for the Service to Shut-Ins. He shares the challenges of the job and how it led to his transfer to the Hyde Park branch.

metadata.title: Service to Shut-Ins and Hyde Park
metadata.synopsis: Arthur Pond talks about his role in the Municipal Reference Library and his transition to working for the Service to Shut-Ins. He shares the challenges of the job and how it led to his transfer to the Hyde Park branch.

lines.line: # Cahuenga Branch and Area Manager Roles

> Arthur Pond discusses his move to the Cahuenga Branch, the renovation of the branch, and his roles as an area manager. He also touches on the political dynamics within the library system and the broader community.

metadata.title: Cahuenga Branch and Area Manager Roles
metadata.synopsis: Arthur Pond discusses his move to the Cahuenga Branch, the renovation of the branch, and his roles as an area manager. He also touches on the political dynamics within the library system and the broader community.

lines.line: # Journey to Librarianship

> Arthur Pond reflects on his path to becoming a librarian, including his work-study jobs and decision to attend library school. He shares his experiences and the thought process that led him to choose librarianship as a career.

metadata.title: Journey to Librarianship
metadata.synopsis: Arthur Pond reflects on his path to becoming a librarian, including his work-study jobs and decision to attend library school. He shares his experiences and the thought process that led him to choose librarianship as a career.

lines.line: # Influential Figures in Career

> The speaker mentions several individuals who had a significant impact on his career, including Fontaine Holmes, Pearl Yonezawa, and Harriet Newton. He acknowledges the influence these figures had on his professional development.

metadata.title: Influential Figures in Career
metadata.synopsis: The speaker mentions several individuals who had a significant impact on his career, including Fontaine Holmes, Pearl Yonezawa, and Harriet Newton. He acknowledges the influence these figures had on his professional development.

lines.line: # Recollection of the Central Library Fire

> Arthur Pond recalls where he was during the Central Library fire and the aftermath of the event. He describes the initial response, the packing of water-soaked books, and the impact on the library system.

metadata.title: Recollection of the Central Library Fire
metadata.synopsis: Arthur Pond recalls where he was during the Central Library fire and the aftermath of the event. He describes the initial response, the packing of water-soaked books, and the impact on the library system.

children.text: # Introduction to the Interview

> The speaker introduces herself as Tiffany Sanford, a librarian at the North Hollywood regional branch of the Los Angeles Public Library, and sets the stage for the interview with Arthur Pond, who is present for the discussion.
# Early Career and Union Involvement

> Arthur Pond recounts the beginning of his career at LAPL as a young adult librarian and his subsequent transfers within the system. He also discusses his early involvement with the union, leading to his eventual role as union president.
# Senior Librarian Exam and Promotion

> The speaker describes taking the senior librarian's exam while ill, the rumors about his score, and the eventual revelation of his actual ranking. Despite the initial disappointment, he later receives a promotion to a senior librarian position.
# Hyde Park Branch and Union Presidency

> The speaker shares his experiences at the Hyde Park branch, including the impact of the LA riots and his tenure as union president. He reflects on the challenges and successes during this period of his career.
# The Great Migration and North Hollywood Retirement

> The speaker talks about the staff shuffling known as the Great Migration and his eventual retirement from the North Hollywood branch. He reflects on the changes in the library system and his own career trajectory.
# Service to Shut-Ins and Hyde Park

> Arthur Pond talks about his role in the Municipal Reference Library and his transition to working for the Service to Shut-Ins. He shares the challenges of the job and how it led to his transfer to the Hyde Park branch.
# Cahuenga Branch and Area Manager Roles

> Arthur Pond discusses his move to the Cahuenga Branch, the renovation of the branch, and his roles as an area manager. He also touches on the political dynamics within the library system and the broader community.
# Journey to Librarianship

> Arthur Pond reflects on his path to becoming a librarian, including his work-study jobs and decision to attend library school. He shares his experiences and the thought process that led him to choose librarianship as a career.
# Influential Figures in Career

> The speaker mentions several individuals who had a significant impact on his career, including Fontaine Holmes, Pearl Yonezawa, and Harriet Newton. He acknowledges the influence these figures had on his professional development.
# Recollection of the Central Library Fire

> Arthur Pond recalls where he was during the Central Library fire and the aftermath of the event. He describes the initial response, the packing of water-soaked books, and the impact on the library system.

story.title: LAPL Institutional Collection - Arthur Pond
story.description: Retired librarian Arthur Pond held several positions over the years, including Librarian, Senior Librarian, Acting Area Manager at many Los Angeles Public Library branches, the Municipal Reference Library, and Service to Shut-Ins. He was interviewed by Librarian Tiffney Sanford at the West Valley Regional Branch Library on October 2, 2024.
